steps.title: Apply the discriminant condition for no real solutions
steps.content: For the equation $-x^2 + bx - 169 = 0$, the quadratic coefficient is $-1$, the linear coefficient is $b$, and the constant is $-169$. A quadratic has no real solutions when its discriminant is negative. Applying that here gives $$b^2-4(-1)(-169)\lt0$$ so $$b^2-676\lt0.$$

steps.title: Solve the inequality for b
steps.content: From $b^2-676\lt0$, get $$b^2\lt676.$$ Since $676=26^2$, this means $$|b|\lt26,$$ or $$-26\lt b\lt26.$$

steps.title: Find the least integer value of b
steps.content: Since $b$ must be an integer with $-26\lt b\lt26$, the possible integer values run from $-25$ through $25$. The least of these is $b=-25$. In Desmos, check the boundary and nearby integer values by evaluating $b^2-676$; negative values give no real solutions, while $b=\pm26$ gives exactly one real solution instead.

steps.title: Confirm the answer
steps.content: The least integer that keeps the discriminant negative is -25.
